title: Insecure Deserialization Detected in JSESSIONID Cookie

steps.message: Block the source IP or detected malicious pattern associated with the 'JSESSIONID' deserialization attempt in the Web Application Firewall (WAF) or network firewall to prevent further exploitation.

Query the SIEM to identify the full request context, including source IP, User-Agent, and any subsequent activities related to the 'JSESSIONID' cookie containing the suspicious serialized object. Identify potentially affected hosts or user accounts.

params.query: index=web sourcetype=access_logs "JSESSIONID=*rO0ABX*" OR "cookie=*rO0ABX*" | table _time, src_ip, user_agent, cookie, uri_path, http_method

steps.message: Isolate the endpoint or server hosting the application that processed the malicious 'JSESSIONID' cookie, if an affected host is identified, to prevent further compromise or lateral movement.

Forcefully terminate all active user sessions for the affected application and invalidate session cookies. If user compromise is suspected based on SIEM analysis, trigger programmatic credential resets for the affected users.

Approve the long-term remediation strategy for the insecure deserialization vulnerability. This may involve deploying a code patch to sanitize or completely avoid deserializing untrusted 'JSESSIONID' content, or implementing a whitelist for acceptable classes. Consider rollback plans.

Notify the Incident Response team and relevant stakeholders about the deserialization incident, detailing containment actions taken and the proposed remediation plan. Create a high-priority incident ticket for tracking.

params.message_text: CRITICAL: Insecure Deserialization detected in 'JSESSIONID' cookie. Immediate containment actions are underway. Please refer to the incident ticket for full details and updates.
